title: Bibijan W/O Dastagirpasha Inamadar vs Abdulrazak S/O Mohammadsab Inamadar ... on 31 August, 2023
body: Bibijan W/O Dastagirpasha Inamadar vs Abdulrazak S/O Mohammadsab Inamadar ... on 31 August, 2023 -1- NC: 2023:KHC-K:6913 RSA No. 7309 of 2012 IN THE HIGH COURT OF KARNATAKA, KALABURAGI BENCH DATED THIS THE 31ST DAY OF AUGUST, 2023 BEFORE THE HON'BLE MRS JUSTICE K S HEMALEKHA REGULAR SECOND APPEAL NO.7309 OF 2012 (PAR) BETWEEN: BIBIJAN W/O DASTAGIRPASHA INAMADAR AGE: 53 YEARS OCC: HOUSEHOLD R/O SHEGUNSHI TQ:BIJAPUR- 586101. ...APPELLANT (BY SRI ANANTH S. JAHAGIRDAR, ADVOCATE) AND: 1. ABDULRAZAK S/O MOHAMMADSAB INAMADAR AGED: 63 YEARS OCC: AGRICULTURE Digitally signed by SHYAMALA R/O UTANAL Location: HIGH TQ: BIJAPUR- 586101. COURT OF KARNATAKA 2. ABDULWAHAB S/O MOHAMMADSAB INAMADAR AGED: 58 YEARS OCC: AGRICULTURE R/O UTANAL TQ: BIJAPUR- 586101. 3. HAFIZAHAMAD S/O MOHAMMADSAB INAMADAR -2- NC: 2023:KHC-K:6913 RSA No. 7309 of 2012 AGED: 56 YEARS OCC: AGRICULTURE R/O UTANAL TQ: BIJAPUR- 586101. 4. RAHIMABI W/O HUSENBEG NAVADGI AGED: 53 YEARS OCC: HOUSEHOLD R/O MANAGULI TQ: BASAVANABAGEWADI- 586101. 5. BENAPPA S/O LACHAPPA KRISHU AGED: MAJOR OCC: AGRICULTURE R/O MANAGULI TQ: BASAVANABAGEWADI- 586101. ...RESPONDENTS (BY SRI R. S. SIDHAPURKAR, ADVOCATE FOR R-1; SRI HARSHAVARDHAN R. MALIPATIL, ADVOCATE FOR R-3; V/O DATED 28.11.2022 NOTICE TO R-2, 4 & 5 ARE DISPENSED WITH ) THIS RSA IS FILED U/S SECTION 100 CPC , PRAYING TO ALLOW THIS APPEAL AND TO SET ASIDE THE JUDGMENT AND DECREE DATED 22.03.2011 PASSED IN R.A.NO.444/2004 ON THE FILE OF FAST TRACK COURT-I/II, BIJAPUR, CONFIRMING THE JUDGMENT AND DECREE DATED 12.06.2022 IN O.S.NO.85/1992 ON THE FILE OF THE PRL. CIVIL JUDGE (JR.DN) BIJAPUR, AND PASS ANY OTHER APPROPRIATE ORDER AS THIS HON'BLE COURT DEEM FIT IN THE CIRCUMSTANCES OF THE CASE, IN THE INTEREST OF JUSTICE. THIS APPEAL COMING ON FOR ADMISSION, THIS DAY, THE COURT DELIVERED THE FOLLOWING: -3- NC: 2023:KHC-K:6913 RSA No. 7309 of 2012 JUDGMENT The present second appeal by the unsuccessful plaintiff assailing the concurrent findings of the courts below dated 22.03.2011 in R.A.No.444/2004 on the file of the Fast Track-II, Bijapur confirming the judgment and decree dated 12.06.2002 in O.S.No.85/1992 on the file of Prl. Judge (Jr.Dn.), Bijapur, whereby, the suit of the plaintiff seeking partition and separate possession of her share in the suit schedule properties was dismissed by the courts below. 2. The parties are referred to as per their rank before the trial court. 3. The family pedigree of the parties to the suit is as under: Hafizahmed (D) Mohammadsab (D) = Mahibubbi (D) Abdulrazak Abdulwahab Bibijan Hafizahamad Rahimabai (D1) (D-2) (Pltf) (D3) (D4) -4- NC: 2023:KHC-K:6913 RSA No. 7309 of 2012 4. The suit of the plaintiff for partition and separate possession claiming her share in the suit schedule properties contending that plaintiff and defendant Nos.1 to 4 are children of one late Mohammadsab and Mahibubbi, who died leaving behind the plaintiff and defendant Nos.1 to 4 as the legal representatives. It is contended that after the demise of her father, the plaintiff herself and defendant Nos.1 to 4 are in joint possession and enjoyment of the suit schedule properties and she has got 1/8th share. 5. The suit was resisted by respondent Nos.1, 3 and 4. The suit against respondent No.2 was dismissed for non-taking steps. Defendant No.1 filed written statement and same was adopted by defendant No.3. The defendant No.4 files separate written statement. 6. The defendant No.1 denied that the plaintiff is entitled for share in the suit properties specifically contending that the suit schedule properties bearing RS -5- NC: 2023:KHC-K:6913 RSA No. 7309 of 2012 No.2702 of Managoli village was measuring 11 acres and it belong to late Mohammadasb Inamdar, the father of the plaintiff and defendant Nos.1 to 4, the said property was given in mehar to Mahibubbi W/o Mohammadsab Inamdar, the mother of plaintiff and defendant Nos.1 to 4 and accordingly, mutation entry was effected. It is stated that the mother of the plaintiff and defendant Nos.1 to 4 gave up her right in respect of aforesaid land survey number 2702 on 31.08.1978 in favour of defendant Nos.1 to 3, pursuant to which the defendant Nos.1 to 3 partitioned the land in three hissas and they are in possession and enjoyment of their respective shares. It is further averred that defendant No.2 who was allotted land in Sy.No.2702/2 has sold his property in favour of defendant No.5 and the defendant No.5 is in actual possession and enjoyment of the said land. It is further averred that during the life time of Mohammadsab, the father of the plaintiff and defendant Nos.1 to 4 was in actual possession of suit land Sy.Nos.23 and 24 of Utnal village and orally gifted the said properties on 21.11.1988 in favour of the -6- NC: 2023:KHC-K:6913 RSA No. 7309 of 2012 defendant Nos.1 to 3 and on the said date itself the possession of the land has been handed over to the defendants and they have become absolute owner of land Sy.Nos.23 and 24. 7. The defendant No.4 filed written statement inter alia supporting the claim of the plaintiff and stating that the plaintiff is entitled for share in the suit schedule properties and admitted the claim of the plaintiff. 8. The trial court on the basis of the pleadings, framed the following issues: "1. Whether the defendants 1 to 3 prove that suit land Sy.No.2702 was given to their mother by way of "Mahar' in the marriage by their father? 2. Whether the defendants 1 to 3 prove the factum and validity of alleged relinquishment of the suit land Sy.No.2702 by their mother Mahaboobi in their favour? -7- NC: 2023:KHC-K:6913 RSA No. 7309 of 2012 3. Whether the defendant No.1 proves that his father Mohamedsab Inamdar has orally gifted away the suit properties R.S.No.23 and 24 in his favour on 21-11- 1988 and as such he is the absolute owner of the same? 4. Whether the defendants 1 to 3 prove that defendant No.2 has sold his share in R.S.No.2702 in favour of Benappa L Kirasur, and as such without impleading the said Benappa, suit is bad for non- joinder?" 5. Whether the plaintiff proves that she is in joint possession of the suit properties, along with defendants?" 6. Whether the plaintiff is entitled for partition and separate possession in the suit properties?" 7. If so what is the share of plaintiff and each of defendants 1 to 4? 8. Whether the defendants 1 to 3 prove that court fee paid is not proper and sufficient? -8- NC: 2023:KHC-K:6913 RSA No. 7309 of 2012 9. Whether the defendants 1 to 3 are entitle for compensation costs? 9. In order to substantiate their claim of the plaintiff, the plaintiff got herself examined as P.W.1 and got marked documents at Exs.P.1 to 7. On the other hand, defendant Nos.1 and 4 got themselves examined as D.Ws.1 and 4 and two witnesses were examined as D.Ws.2 and 3 and got marked Exs.D1 to 8 on their behalf. 10. The trial court based on the pleadings oral and documentary evidence held as under: i) Defendant Nos.1 to 3 proved that suit land bearing R.S.No.2702 was given to them by way of Mehar and their mother Mehaboobi has relinquished the suit land Sy.No.2702 in their favour. ii) That the defendant No.1 proved that his father Mohammadsab Inamdar gifted the suit land R.S.Nos.23 and 24 on 21.11.1988 and he is the absolute owner of the same. -9- NC: 2023:KHC-K:6913 RSA No. 7309 of 2012 Holding so, the trial court by its judgment and decree dismissed the suit of the plaintiff. 11. Aggrieved by the which, the plaintiff preferred regular appeal. The first appellate, after re-analyzing and re-appreciating both oral and documentary evidence on record placed before it, has concurred with the findings of the trial court. Against the concurrent findings of the courts below, the present appeal is filed by the plaintiff. 12. It is pertinent to note here that, the learned counsel for the respondents has filed a memo seeking dismissal of the appeal insofar as item Nos.2(5) and (6) contending that, during the pendency of the appeal the said properties have been sold by respondent No.1- defendant No.1 in favour of one Tippanna by way of registered sale deed dated 28.01.2012, to the said sale deed plaintiff and defendant Nos.2 to 4 are the signatories and would contend that the plaintiff having accepted the sale deed, the present appeal insofar as suit schedule item No.2(5) and (6) needs to be dismissed. - 10 - NC: 2023:KHC-K:6913 RSA No. 7309 of 2012 13. The said document i.e., sale deed placed before the court would be of less consequence, since the alleged sale deed is executed during the pendency of the appeal and if any right or any challenge has to be made to the same, that would be in accordance with law, at this stage, the said document cannot be taken into consideration, thus, this court observed accordingly. 14. Heard Sri Ananth S. Jahagirdar, learned counsel appearing for the plaintiff and Sri R.S. Sidhapurkar, learned counsel for respondent No.1 and Sri Harshavardhan R. Malipatil, learned counsel for respondent No.3. 15. Learned counsel for the appellant would contend that, in the absence of any relinquishment deed made by the mother of the plaintiff and defendant Nos.1 to 4, the courts below were not justified in holding that there is relinquishment in favour of defendant Nos.1 to 3 in respect of suit land bearing R.S.No.2702 and the suit of the plaintiff was erroneously dismissed by the courts - 11 - NC: 2023:KHC-K:6913 RSA No. 7309 of 2012 below. The learned counsel would contend that the courts below have committed grave error in holding that the oral gift deed in respect of land bearing R.S.Nos.23 and 24 by the father of the plaintiff and defendant Nos.1 to 4 has been proved without there being proof of factum of gift, handing over of possession and acceptance of the gift and the oral gift having not been proved in accordance with law as envisaged under the principles of Mohammadan Law, the courts below were not justified in dismissing the suit of the plaintiff. It is contended by the learned counsel for the appellant that there arises a substantial question of law for consideration, more particularly, the substantial questions of law that is framed by the appellant in the appeal memorandum. 16. Per contra, learned counsel for the respondents would justify the judgment and decree of the trial court and would contend that the relinquishment made by the mother is admittedly a Mehar property in favour of defendant Nos.1 to 3. The terms of gift as envisaged - 12 - NC: 2023:KHC-K:6913 RSA No. 7309 of 2012 under Section 138 of the Mohammadan Law is applicable since the parties are Mohammadan, in light of which registration of gift is not required under the said law. The learned counsel would justify the judgment and decree of the courts below and contend that land in Sy.Nos.23 and 24 being the absolute property of the father of the plaintiff and defendant Nos.1 to 4 and the gift deed was acted upon during his life time, the ingredients as envisaged to prove the gift deed under Mohammadan Law having proved, the courts below were justified in dismissing the suit of the plaintiff and there arises no substantial question of law to be considered in the present appeal and sought for dismissal of the appeal. 17. This court has carefully considered the rival contentions urged by the learned counsel for the parties and perused the material on record including the judgment and decrees of the courts below. 18. The undisputed facts are that: - 13 - NC: 2023:KHC-K:6913 RSA No. 7309 of 2012 i) The suit land bearing R.S.No.2702 was a Mehar property of Mahibubbi W/o Mohammadsab Inamdar, the mother of the plaintiff and defendant Nos.1 to 4. ii) Land bearing RS.Nos.23 and 24 were the absolute properties of the father of the plaintiff and defendant Nos.1 to 4. iii) The plaintiff in her plaint only averred that she is entitled for 1/8th share in the suit schedule properties. iv) The defendants contended that the mother of the plaintiff and defendant Nos.1 to 4 had relinquished the land bearing R.S.No.2702 in favour of defendant Nos.1 to 3 and they are in peaceful possession and enjoyment of their respective shares which they had subsequently partitioned among themselves by three divisions. - 14 - NC: 2023:KHC-K:6913 RSA No. 7309 of 2012 v) The defendants further contended that oral gift has been executed by their father Mohammadsab in respect of land bearing RS Nos.23 and 24 and they have been in possession of the said properties. Since the parties are Mohammadans, the relevant provisions that would be applicable is Section 138 of Chapter-XI of Mulla's Principles of Mahomedan Law, which defines Hiba or gift which reads as under: "138. Hiba or gift-. A hiba or gift is "a transfer of property, made immediately, and without any exchange," by one person to another, and accepted by or on behalf of the latter. Whether a document is a gift deed or a will, can be gathered from the recitals in the document. Even the title given to it is not conclusive of its true nature. Therefore, the terms, conditions and recitals alone determine the nature of the disposition. They are to be taken as a whole. Where the disposition of the right, title and interest accrues in praesenti it cannot be treated as will because in a will the disposition is carried into effect after the death of the maker. When once it is clear from the recitals that the ownership - 15 - NC: 2023:KHC-K:6913 RSA No. 7309 of 2012 has been transferred in praesenti absolute it is a gift and any condition imposed on the enjoyment of the property is invalid. The gift must be accepted and completed by such delivery of possession as the nature of the property admits (a)." 19. The pre-requisite condition of a gift as envisaged under Section 138 is that the gift must be accepted and acted by delivery of possession as contemplated under Section 122 of the Transfer of Property Act, 1882, which reads as under: " 122. "Gift" defined.--"Gift" is the transfer of certain existing moveable or immoveable property made voluntarily and without consideration, by one person, called the donor, to another, called the donee, and accepted by or on behalf of the donee. Acceptance when to be made.--Such acceptance must be made during the lifetime of the donor and while he is still capable of giving. If the donee dies before acceptance, the gift is void." 20. Though the term used by the defendants is a relinquishment in favour of defendant Nos.1 to 3 in respect of land bearing RS No.2702 by the mother of the - 16 - NC: 2023:KHC-K:6913 RSA No. 7309 of 2012 plaintiff and defendant Nos.1 to 3, the said property is a gift in favour of defendant Nos.1 to 3 during her life time as contended by the defendants. Admittedly, the defendants are in possession of the suit schedule property of the said survey number and pursuant to which defendant No.2, the property which was allotted in his favour was sold to defendant No.5. The pre-condition of the gift and delivery of possession has been established by the defendant Nos.1 to 3 in respect to Sy.No.2702. It is the case of the defendants that during the life time of their parents, the brothers had partitioned the suit properties and the entire property in Sy.No.2702 standing in the name of plaintiff's mother Mehabubbi was partitioned long back and defendants were put in possession of the said property. The oral and documentary evidence produced before the trial court reveals that the mother of the plaintiff and defendant Nos.1 to 4 had received the land in Sy.No.2702 by way of Mehar and she has relinquished/gifted her rights in favour of defendant Nos.1 and 3. - 17 - NC: 2023:KHC-K:6913 RSA No. 7309 of 2012 21. The next point to be considered is regarding oral gift in respect of Sy.Nos.23 and 24 under Ex.D.8 i.e. oral gift deed dated 21.11.1988 which is said to be executed by Mohammadsab in favour of defendant No.1. 22. In order to prove the oral gift, the following are the necessary requisites: i) Declaration of gift by the donor. ii) Acceptance of gift expressed or implied by or on behalf of donee. iii) Delivery of the possession of the gift by donor to the donee. 23. In the present facts of the case, the declaration during the life time of the father has been made by way of a mutation entry, the acceptance of gift on behalf of defendant No.1, by delivery of possession and hence all the three ingredients have been fulfilled in the present case and thus the contention of the appellant-plaintiff that the defendants have failed to establish the oral gift in their favour is unsustainable. The trial court, before whom the - 18 - NC: 2023:KHC-K:6913 RSA No. 7309 of 2012 entire evidence was conducted, has come to the conclusion that the plaintiff has failed to establish her right to claim 1/8th in the suit schedule properties when the properties have been relinquished by the mother and oral gift by father in favour of defendant Nos.1 to 3. 24. The first appellate court which is the last fact finding court has re-analyzed and re-appreciated the entire evidence and material on record and has confirmed the judgment and decree of the trial court, holding that during the life time of the parents of the plaintiff and defendant Nos.1 to 4, the father of the plaintiff has orally gifted land Sy.Nos.23 and 24 in favour of defendant No.1 and the property bearing Sy.No.2702 which is the Mehar property of mother of plaintiff and defendant Nos.1 to 4 said to have been relinquished, which is nothing but an oral gift in favour of the plaintiff and defendant Nos.1 to 3 and the same is evidenced by material documents which have been placed before the trial court at Exs.D.1 to 8. - 19 - NC: 2023:KHC-K:6913 RSA No. 7309 of 2012 25. In light of the above, the manner in which the courts below have considered the entire evidence and material placed before it, this court is of the considered view that same does not call for any interference and there arises no substantial question of law for consideration to be dealt with under Section 100 CPC. Hence, this court pass the following: ORDER i) The regular second appeal filed by the plaintiff is hereby dismissed. ii) The impugned judgments and decrees of the courts below stand confirmed. iii) No order as to costs in the facts and circumstances of the case. Sd/- JUDGE BL List No.: 1 Sl No.: 37 Ct:NS
